data.prompt_text: Two hikers at points B and C are 494 feet apart on a flat plain. They are looking at a landmark at A. They measure \angle B = 38.7^\circ and \angle C = 101.7^\circ. What is the distance from hiker B to the landmark (distance AB)?
data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 494 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 38.7^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 101.7^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 38.7^\circ - 101.7^\circ = 39.6^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(101.7^\circ)} = \frac{494}{\sin(39.6^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{494\sin(101.7^\circ)}{\sin(39.6^\circ)}

c \approx 758.9

c \approx 41.7

\text{We are given side } a = 144 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 62.7^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 20.7^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 62.7^\circ - 20.7^\circ = 96.6^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(20.7^\circ)} = \frac{144}{\sin(96.6^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{144\sin(20.7^\circ)}{\sin(96.6^\circ)}

c \approx 51.2

\frac{c}{\sin(72.5^\circ)} = \frac{33}{\sin(29.6^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{33\sin(72.5^\circ)}{\sin(29.6^\circ)}

c \approx 63.7

\text{We are given side } a = 30 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 62.0^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 20.8^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 62.0^\circ - 20.8^\circ = 97.2^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(20.8^\circ)} = \frac{30}{\sin(97.2^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{30\sin(20.8^\circ)}{\sin(97.2^\circ)}

c \approx 10.7

data.prompt_text: A surveyor wants to find the distance across a river from point B to point A. They lay out a baseline BC of 395 meters on one side of the river. They measure the angles \angle B = 113.8^\circ and \angle C = 38.2^\circ. Find the distance across the river from point B to point A.
data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 395 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 113.8^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 38.2^\circ.

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(38.2^\circ)} = \frac{395}{\sin(28.0^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{395\sin(38.2^\circ)}{\sin(28.0^\circ)}

c \approx 520.3
